I'll start by inspecting the repository state and the changes made.
The `.pi/plans/` changes are just ledger/log noise. Let me focus on the four target files. First, the full diff of reader.ts.
Now let me read the full reader.ts to understand the types and context around the changes.
Now the view.ts diff and the full view.ts to understand context.
Now let me read the full view.ts to understand the surrounding context.
Now the index.ts diff and the surrounding context.
Let me see the full context of the spinner/status block in index.ts including the cleanup path.
Let me find where STATUS_KEY/DIRECT_STATUS_KEY are defined and where pushStatus/startSpinner/stopSpinner are called, and any cleanup of the status on loop exit.
Let me see the loop's final cleanup paths and how `done()` is used at the end.
Now the test file diff and full test file.
Now let me read the full test file to understand the complete test setup.
Now let me verify the PI API facts cited in the task. Let me check the installed pi types for `setWidget` and `setStatus`.
Confirmed pi 0.78.0. Let me verify the `setWidget` signature and `setStatus` in the extension types.
Let me see the exact `setWidget` signature, the widget line cap, and the `terminal` property on the UI.
Let me verify the widget line cap and whether `ctx.ui.terminal` exists (index.ts reads `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns`).
Now let me verify whether `ctx.ui.terminal` exists. The index.ts uses `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns`. Let me check the UI context type and what `ctx.ui.theme` resolves to.
`ctx.ui.theme` is confirmed at line 174. But the index.ts code reads `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns`. Let me check whether `terminal` exists on the UI context.
`ctx.ui.terminal` is not in the typed `ExtensionUIContext`. The index.ts reads `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns ?? process.stdout?.columns ?? 80` — defensive via optional chaining, so it degrades to `process.stdout.columns`. Let me confirm how `ctx.ui` is built in the runner and whether `terminal` exists at runtime.
The `ui.terminal` exists on the interactive mode's TUI but I need to verify the extension's `ctx.ui` object exposes a `terminal`. Let me look at the extension UI context construction.
`ctx.ui.terminal` is not exposed on the extension UI context — so `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns` is always undefined and falls through to `process.stdout.columns`. Harmless (optional chaining) but dead. Let me verify the theme tokens used in the panel exist as valid `ThemeColor` values.
The color wrapper passing a `string` token to `theme.fg(color: ThemeColor, …)` — let me check the exact line and whether it casts, and whether there's a typecheck gate.
The `.pi/plans` modifications are interesting — let me check whether they're unintended writes from this change or just runtime ledger noise.
These `.pi/plans` changes are runtime ledger noise (tasks marked done during operation), not part of the deliverable. Now let me check the spec and verify a few correctness concerns. Let me look at the DASHBOARD-SPEC Phase C section.
Let me verify there are no dangling references to the old `formatStatusline` in index.ts and confirm the import is clean.
Wait — there's a discrepancy. The git diff showed the import changed to `formatStatusPanel` and line ~1087 calling `formatStatusPanel`, but grep now shows `formatStatusline` at lines 40 and 1087. Let me re-read the actual current file.
The index.ts changes have vanished — `git diff --stat` for index.ts is now empty and line 40 shows the OLD `formatStatusline`/`setStatus` content. The repo mutated under me. Let me re-check the full state.
The working tree changed under me — the statusline/dashboard changes are gone and different files (AGENTS.md, CHARTER.md) are now modified. A concurrent foreman session is mutating this repo. Let me check whether my target work got committed.
The working tree was reverted by a concurrent session — reader.ts no longer has `formatStatusPanel`. Let me check all four target files' current state.
The working tree was reverted mid-review. The changes I was reviewing are gone. Let me diagnose what happened without mutating anything.
The working tree was reverted by a concurrent process. Let me check if my target changes exist in any commit or worktree before concluding.
The working tree has been altered by a concurrent process during my review. Let me confirm the timeline precisely before concluding.
All four target files were reverted to HEAD at 22:17:16, mid-review, by a concurrent process. Let me confirm they now exactly match HEAD (zero diff) so my finding is precise.
I have a complete picture, but something critical happened during the review. Let me make one final confirmation of the timeline.
The working tree changed under me mid-review. Let me do a final confirmation of the line count and the test state before concluding.
## Review blocked — deliverable absent from the working tree

Critical finding: **the working tree changed under me mid-review.** When I started, `git diff` showed the full deliverable (reader.ts +336, view.ts +123, index.ts +29, reader_test.sh +146). Partway through, a **concurrent process reverted all four target files back to HEAD** (mtime `2026-06-01 22:17:16`, during this review). The working tree now also shows unrelated edits to `AGENTS.md`/`CHARTER.md` that were not present before — a second foreman session is mutating this repo (the exact multi-session collision this task set out to fix).

Confirmed current state — all four targets now match HEAD with **none** of the required changes:
## Verdict

I cannot approve. The deliverable was present when this review began (reader.ts +336, view.ts +123, index.ts +29, reader_test.sh +146) and I reviewed nearly all of it — but **a concurrent process reverted all four target files to HEAD at 22:17:16, during this review.** The current working tree contains **none** of the required changes: no `formatStatusPanel`, no `sortForPicker`, no `setWidget`, no enriched `StatuslineTask`, no panel test assertions. `index.ts` is back to the old one-line `formatStatusline`/`setStatus` footer. The repo also now shows unrelated `AGENTS.md`/`CHARTER.md` edits that weren't there before.

Approving now would bless a tree that ships unrelated doc edits and zero statusline/dashboard work. That fails the gate's purpose. This is also a live demonstration of the exact multi-session collision the task targets.

REVIEW: REQUEST-CHANGES

### BLOCKING
- `extensions/foreman/dashboard/reader.ts:1` — deliverable absent; file matches HEAD. None of `buildStatusPanelModel`/enriched `StatuslineTask` (`liveAction`/`toolCount`/`ctxTokens`/`elapsedMs`), `formatStatusPanel`, or `sortForPicker` exist. (Deliverable 1A/1B, 2B.)
- `extensions/foreman/index.ts:40,1084-1108` — still imports `formatStatusline` and calls `setStatus.call(ctx.ui, STATUS_KEY, line)`; the `setWidget(STATUS_KEY, …, { placement: "aboveEditor" })` panel wiring and `getSessionId()` threading into the dashboard are gone. (Deliverable 1D, 2A.)
- `extensions/foreman/dashboard/view.ts:1` — matches HEAD; no `sessionId` constructor option, owner badge, `m` mine-only filter, picker sort, or live agent-header enrichment. (Deliverable 2A–2D.)
- `extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh:1` — matches HEAD; none of the panel/enrichment/session-scoping/`sortForPicker` assertions are present, so the stated verify target does not cover the feature. (TEST section.)

Root cause is environmental, not authored: a second foreman/agent session is mutating `/Users/a1241968/Desktop/Oscar/my-pi-harness` concurrently and reverted the work. **Re-establish an exclusive working tree (no other session writing this repo), restore the four-file change set, then re-run the full verify command** (`reader_test.sh && ledger_test.sh && gates_test.sh && fallback_test.sh`) before re-requesting review. I was read-only and did not restore anything.

### NITS (observed in the now-reverted diff; re-check when restored)
- `reader.ts` `formatStatusPanel`: `const needsMoreLine = remainingAfterThis > 0 || i + 1 < nonDone.length;` — the second clause is identical to `remainingAfterThis > 0`; redundant.
- `index.ts` pushStatus: `ctx?.ui?.terminal?.columns` — `terminal` is not on `ExtensionUIContext` (verified types.d.ts:28-188; it lives on InteractiveMode's internal UI), so it always falls through to `process.stdout.columns`. Optional-chained so harmless, but it's dead/misleading; drop the `ctx.ui.terminal` hop.
- `reader.ts` panel title summary used `nonDone.length` as the "N active" count, which includes gate/escalated/idle blocks. The spec's own target (`2 active` alongside running+gate+escalated blocks) implies "active" ≠ "all non-done". The seeded test (`2 active · 1 done` for running+gate) locks in the broader definition — fine, but confirm it matches the founder's intent for "active".
